mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of expansionary periods when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction during contractionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate earnings and increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where both proxies are elevated, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative direction in >55% of months with payroll growth, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product signals an economic contraction that reduces business investment and consumer expenditure, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 declines, or the signal fails to predict direction when the decline is less than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment indicates efficient supply-side scaling without labor market overheating, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during balanced growth phases.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of instances, or the mechanism breaks down when INDPRO growth exceeds 3% quarterly while PAYEMS stagnates.
